g.text: Rules, Terms and Conditions
My funeral policy
Your policy comes into being when we, or someone authorised by us, receives
your acceptance form (on the back of this page) and the first premium for your
policy. Your acceptance form and the policy provisions on this page are a
complete summary of your policy.
The life insured
This is the person whose life is insured under your policy. This is you and
anyone you include as your family or extended family, and in whom you have
an insurable interest.
The start date for the life insured
This is the first day of the month in which we, or someone authorised by us,
receives the first premium for the life insured. That’s when death cover for an
accidental death starts, as well as the waiting period for a natural
(non-accidental) death for the payment of the full benefit. During a waiting
period, premiums must be paid, but policy benefits cannot be claimed.
Waiting periods
Accidental death - No waiting period applies and the benefit will be paid in full
provided the first premium has been received.
Age of lives insured at their start date
You and your spouse must be under the age of 100 years. Children included as
immediate family must be under the age 22, children who are permanently disabled
and totally dependant on you may be included at any age, and children who are
unmarried and full-time students at an education institution approved by us may be
included if they are under the age of 25. Extended family, including a spouse or
children included as extended family must be under the age of 100.
When cover for children stops
Cover for children stops when they turn 22, for full-time students (described
above),
it stops when they turn 25, and for disabled children (described
above), it does not
stop at any specific age. For a still-born child, the death
benefit will be paid only if the
birth takes place after 26 weeks of pregnancy,
and is not self-induced.
Maximum funeral cover
When a person takes out funeral insurance with us, the total funeral cover for
that
person under all individual and group policies with us may not be more
than the
following maximums; R10 000 if the funeral insurance is taken out
when the person is
less than 6 years old, R25 000 if it is taken out when they
are 6 or older, but younger
than 18, R50 000 if it is taken out when they are 18
or older, but younger than 65, and
R20 000 if they, are 65 and older. Any cover
above the maximum automatically falls
away.
Premiums are paid monthly
Your insurance premium must be paid before the end of each month, and
provides
the death cover for that month. The premium pays for your cover only,
not for any
investment or cash value. Premiums are not refunded if your policy
end for any
reason. Any joining and other fees that you pay under a group
scheme are not part of
the premium. If a premium is not paid on time, and
remains unpaid for another 15
days, your policy ends automatically. If, within 3
months of your policy ending, we
again receive a premium, we may reinstate
your policy and new waiting periods
would then apply. Depending on your plan
chosen commission will be 15% - it’s included in your monthly premium for your
intermediary.
How to claim on this policy
We must receive a completed claim form, obtainable from us, within 6 months
of the
death of the life insured. The person who is claiming must, at their own
cost, prove
the claim, show that no exclusions apply, and give us all
information and documents
needed to consider the claim. This must include
the following:
the claim form properly completed and signed
certified copy of the ID document of the person who is claiming
certified copy of the original death certificate of the life insured (DHA 5)
certified copy of the ID document of the life insured, with “deceased” or a
similar notice stamped on it
copy of the DHA 1663 notification/registration - of - death form
copy of the claimant’s bank statement
police report if the death is due to unnatural causes - for example, a
motor-accident report.
Please take note:
Should the Funeral service not be conducted by Martin’s
Funerals and the client opted to make use of another funeral parlour instead,
the policy holder agrees that a 30% admin fee may be deducted for handling
the claim and payout to the client.
Main Member Signature:
When the policy does not pay out
No benefits are paid out on the policy if the claim caused or accelerated,
directly or
indirectly, by - war, riot commotion or terrorist activity, - willful
exposure to danger, -
involvement in a criminal activity, - radioactivity or
nuclear explosion, - use of alcohol
above the legal limit, - use of any illegal
substance.
You can cancel at any time
You can cancel the policy at any time by written notice. If we receive your
notice no
more than 30 days after you receive this document, the policy ends
when we receive
your notice. This is known as a cooling-off cancellation. If we
receive your notice
after 30 days, your policy will end at the end of the month
in which we receive your
notice, and then only any premiums received after the
policy has end will be
refunded.
The actuarial basis of your policy
Your policy operates according to actuarial rules, specifications are formulae,
which
are approved according to law. These ensure that your policy is
financially sound,
and is run properly and fairly.
For more information, or to claim
Visit any of our offices countrywide. If there is anything you are not happy with
regarding your policy or our services, please contact; 1, Martin’s Funerals
at
info@martinsdirect.com or tel 0860 911 777; 2, our complaints
Department at
info@martinsdirect.com or fax 011 412 1985. You may also
contact the:
Compliance Department
If you feel that African Unity Life has in any way not complied with statutory or
regulatory requirements, please contact the compliance office. Complaints
that are
not resolved to your satisfaction may be referred to: Ombudsman for
Long-term
Insurance at: P.O. Box 45007, CLAREMONT, 7735, Tel 021 657
0951, Email info@ombud.co.za. Registrar for Long-term
Insurance at: P.O. Box
info@fsb.co.za. FAIS Ombud at: P.O. Box 74571, LYNWOOD
RIDGE, 0040, Tel 012
info@faisombud.co.za.
Declaration of Understanding:
1. The agent has provided me with satisfactory details of his/her relevant
experience and assisted me in completing the application form and in
understanding all the terms and conditions as stipulated in this document.
2. I undertake to abide by these terms and conditions.
The benefits of this policy have been explained in detail to me and I both
understand and desire such benefits.
3. The monthly premium as indicated on this page is affordable within my
current monthly budget.
4. The monthly premium shown includes all commissions, fees and
administration charges allowed in terms of legislation. Details of the
amounts are available on request.
5. I accept and understand the contents of this declaration and attest thereto
by my signature overleaf.
Please Note:
A policyholder having a policy benefit expressed otherwise than
as a sum of money, has the right to request such benefit be paid as a sum of
money
(Section 53 LTIA)
Burial Repatriation Benefit:
Repatriation of mortal remains within South Africa, Namibia, Lesotho,
Swaziland, Botswana, Mozambique and Zimbabwe.
When a member’s death occurs more that 100km from their normal place of
residence/place of burial, the deceased will be transported to the place of
burial irrespective of where the death occurred, or where the burial will take
place, provided that the repatriation is within the defined territory. To the
Maximum of R 15 000 including VAT
Claims Procedure:
In the event of a claim, please contact Martin’s Funerals on 0860 911 777. Claim
forms will be forwarded to you - please complete these forms and
fax back to the claims department on 011 412 1985. The claims department will
notify you of the status of your claim. All premium payments must have been
paid up to date at the time of the claim arising. African Unity Life requires the
following certified documents in order to pay out a claim: i) death certificate ii)
copy of deceased’s ID iii) copy of beneficiary’s ID iv) burial order and v) Police
report in the event of unnatural or accidental cause of death.
Complaint Procedure:
1. Complaint must be in writing and can be submitted any business day to
the administrator.
2. Complainant must disclose interest in the policy, give his/her ID number
and provide the policy number: must disclose nature of the complaint.
3. If not satisfied, complainant can refer the matter to the Ombudsman for
Long-term Insurance or the Registrar for Long-term Insurance.
Replacement of Policies:
Replacement of any insurance is generally to the disadvantage of the proposer
because it involves duplication of initial costs charged to the policy. The
Policyholder should be fully aware of the possible detrimental consequences
of the replacement of an insurance policy. If a replacement is applicable, a
Replacement Policy Advice Record should have been completed by the
advisor of the new business.
Cooling off period:
A Policyholder has the right to cancel a policy within 30
days after receipt of the policy documentation. Please notify the service office
in writing of cancellation - any premium that has been paid (less administration
fee) will be refunded and your policy will be cancelled.
(Please ensure that you retain a copy of this document)
Outstanding premium during waiting period:
Payments of premiums during waiting period must be continuous (ie no
payment can be outstanding or paid late). If a payment is outstanding or paid
late, the policy will lapse and the applicable waiting period/s will start from the
date that payments are resumed.
Underwritten by African Unity Life Limited an Authorised Financial Services
Provider FSP 8447. Riesling House, The Vineyards Office Estate, 99 Jip de Jager,
Bellville, 7530
